info.title: AapadBandhav Smart Emergency Response API
info.description:
## AapadBandhav Platform API

**AapadBandhav** is a Smart Emergency Response and Accident Detection Platform for India.

### Features
- 🚨 Real-time accident detection via IoT crash sensors
- 🚑 Smart dispatch to hospitals, ambulances, police, fire departments
- 🗺️ Live GPS tracking of responders and affected users
- 📲 OTP-based authentication for all entity types
- 🛡️ Full RBAC with 11 roles

### Authentication
All protected endpoints require a **Bearer JWT token**.

To authenticate:
1. Send OTP → `POST /api/auth/otp/send`
2. Verify OTP → `POST /api/auth/otp/verify` → get **token**
3. Click **Authorize** above → enter: `Bearer <your-token>`

For admin access use `POST /api/auth/admin/login`.

### Roles
`user` · `volunteer` · `fire_department` · `emergency_personnel`  
`hospital` · `ambulance` · `police_station` · `policeman`  
`mechanic` · `insurance` · `admin` · `superadmin`

BearerAuth.description: Enter your JWT token. Get one from POST /api/auth/otp/verify or POST /api/auth/admin/login

body.example: An accident has been detected near your location.

post.description: Sends a 6-digit OTP via SMS to the provided mobile number. Rate limited to one OTP per 60 seconds.

429.description: Rate limited – OTP already sent recently

post.description:
Verifies the OTP and issues a JWT token. If the mobile is registered under multiple roles,
returns `needs_role_selection: true` with available roles. If the mobile is new, returns
`is_new_user: true` to direct the user to registration.

role.description: Preferred role when mobile has multiple registrations

200.description: Login successful, new user, or role selection required

post.summary: Register new citizen user via OTP
post.description: Creates a new user account after OTP verification. The OTP must have been sent to the same mobile number. Returns a JWT token on success.

Allows partners (hospital, ambulance, police_station, policeman, mechanic, insurance,
fire_department, volunteer) to register themselves. OTP must be verified first.
The account is created with isActive=false and requires admin approval before login.
Authenticates system administrators and sub-admins using email and password. Returns a JWT token with admin/superadmin role.

get.description: Returns the full profile of the entity associated with the JWT token. Works for all 12 entity types.

post.description: Invalidates the session. Since tokens are stateless JWT, this is a client-side operation; the server returns a success confirmation.

get.description: Returns the full profile of the authenticated entity (works for all 12 entity types).

put.description: Updates profile fields for the authenticated entity. Accepts any combination of profile fields.

get.description: Returns profile for user/volunteer/fire_department roles. Alias for GET /api/profile.

put.description: Updates user profile. Alias for PUT /api/profile.

get.description: Returns all emergency contacts for the authenticated user.

post.description: Creates a new emergency contact for the authenticated user.

put.description: Updates an existing emergency contact by ID.

delete.description: Deletes an emergency contact by ID.

post.description: Manually reports an emergency accident. Triggers the full dispatch pipeline (alerts, route assignment, Inngest workflow). Deduplicates within 5 minutes.

200.description: Accident logged and dispatch pipeline initiated

get.description: Returns all accidents associated with the authenticated user's account.
Returns all accidents. Responders see accidents relevant to their area. Admins see all.

post.description: Cancels an active accident report.
Marks a reported accident as a false alarm and closes it.
Marks an accident as resolved and closes the dispatch pipeline.
Allows responders and admins to update the incident status.

get.description: Returns a chronological log of all status changes for an accident.
Returns full accident details including telemetry, status, and responder information.
Returns enriched accident data including linked alerts, routes, chat messages, and evidence files.

post.description: Submits a structured incident report from a responder after reaching the scene.

get.description: Returns all chat messages in the accident's coordination channel.

post.summary: Send chat message to accident channel
post.description: Sends a coordination message to the accident's real-time chat channel via Pusher.

get.summary: Get recommended responders for an accident
get.description: Returns a scored and sorted list of available responders (hospitals, ambulances, police) near the accident location.

post.summary: Manually assign a responder to an accident
post.description: Dispatches a specific responder to the accident and creates the navigation route.

get.description:
Returns all dispatch alerts sent to the authenticated entity. Works for all responder types:
- `hospital` → `/api/hospitals/alerts`
- `ambulance` → `/api/ambulances/alerts`
- `police_station` → `/api/police/station/alerts`
- `mechanic` → `/api/mechanics/alerts`
- `fire_department` → `/api/fire/alerts`
- `volunteer` → `/api/volunteer/alerts`

200.description: List of alerts for this entity

get.description: Returns full details of a specific alert.

get.summary: Get all alerts for an accident
get.description: Returns all dispatch alerts sent to responders for a specific accident.

post.description:
Accepts or rejects a dispatch alert. On acceptance:
- Creates a navigation route from responder to accident location
- Updates accident status to 'dispatched'
- Broadcasts via Pusher to all accident subscribers

Available on all role-specific paths: `/api/hospitals/alerts/:id/respond`, `/api/ambulances/alerts/:id/respond`, etc.

eta.description: Estimated time of arrival in minutes

get.description: Returns all IoT devices owned by or shared with the authenticated user.

post.description: Links an IoT device to the authenticated user's account using the QR code credentials.

404.description: Device not found or invalid credentials

post.description: Grants another user access to the authenticated user's device.
Removes a user's shared access to a device.

get.summary: List users this device is shared with
get.description: Returns all active shares for the specified device.

get.summary: Get device travel stops / trip history
get.description: Returns a history of stops and trips recorded by the device.

put.description: Updates the display name / vehicle label for the device.

get.description: Returns raw telemetry data logged by the IoT device.

post.summary: Receive IoT device telemetry (MQTT over HTTP bridge)
post.description:
Receives MQTT-format telemetry from IoT crash sensors via HTTP bridge.
Processes GPS position, speed, impact G-force readings, and battery status.

**Auto-Crash Detection:** If `impactValue >= 3.0G`, automatically triggers an accident
and initiates the full emergency dispatch pipeline (Inngest workflow, Pusher broadcast,
alert dispatch to nearby responders).

**Topic Format:** `vehicle/{deviceId}/{nodeId}`
- `nodeId` identifies the physical sensor (front, rear, side)

**Authentication:** No JWT required — authenticated via device credentials embedded in the topic/payload.
Broadcasts the authenticated entity's current GPS position to all subscribers via Pusher. Used by both users and responders during active incidents.

get.summary: Get all active responders on the live map
get.description: Returns current GPS positions of all responders (hospitals, ambulances, police, mechanics, volunteers) who have updated their location in the last 30 minutes.

200.description: List of active responders with positions

get.summary: Get location of a specific entity
get.description: Returns the current GPS position and status of a specific responder or user.

put.description: Updates the responder's availability status (available/busy/offline) without changing their GPS position.

post.summary: Create navigation route for a responder
post.description: Creates a navigation route from a responder's location to the accident scene. Called automatically when a responder accepts an alert.

get.description: Returns the full navigation route including waypoints and ETA.

put.description: Updates the responder's live position while navigating to the accident. Broadcasts update via Pusher.

post.description: Marks the navigation route as completed when the responder arrives at the scene.

get.description: Returns the last 50 in-app notifications for the authenticated entity.

post.description: Stores the Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) device token for the authenticated entity, enabling push notifications on accident and dispatch events.

get.description: Returns all active customers linked to the authenticated insurance company.

post.description: Associates a user with the authenticated insurance company by their mobile number.

delete.description: Deactivates the insurance relationship with a customer.

get.summary: Get accident alerts involving insurance customers
get.description: Returns all emergency alerts triggered by vehicles belonging to the insurance company's customers.

post.description: Generates multiple IoT devices with unique device IDs, QR credentials, and SIM codes. Used for device provisioning.

get.description: Returns full inventory of all IoT devices with status, assignment, and telemetry info.
Returns all service accounts that have been self-registered and are awaiting admin approval (isActive=false, mobileVerified=true).

post.summary: Reject and delete a pending partner application

get.description: Returns key platform metrics including total accidents, active incidents, responder counts, and device status.
Returns detailed analytics including accident trends, response time metrics, SLA compliance, and severity breakdown.

post.description:
Creates a new service provider account for any of the supported roles:
`hospital`, `ambulance`, `police_station`, `policeman`, `mechanic`, `insurance`, `volunteer`, `fire_department`, `emergency_personnel`.

The account is created in inactive state. The service provider activates it on first OTP login.

get.description: Returns paginated list of all registered user accounts with filters.
Returns a quick summary of platform stats (accident counts, user counts, device counts) for dashboard widgets.
